title: Mozilla publishes Mythos harness as verification compounds - TCR 05/08/26
description:
Mozilla published the engineering postmortem for how Anthropic's Claude Mythos found 271 real vulnerabilities in unreleased Firefox 150 code with almost no false positives, Oasis Security disclosed a CVSS 9.7 vulnerability in Cline letting any website hijack AI coding agents through unauthenticated localhost WebSockets, and a Lancet audit screening 2.5 million biomedical papers found 12 times more publications with fabricated citations in 2025 than in 2023.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-8-2026/

0:00 Mozilla Mythos engineering postmortem
2:57 Cline Kanban WebSocket vulnerability
6:05 OpenAI Trusted Contact and relationship safety
8:27 Lancet biomedical citation audit

This episode covers the Mozilla Mythos engineering postmortem and what a reusable AI audit harness looks like, a Tohoku University clinical trial showing a common inexpensive drug slows chronic kidney disease through a newly mapped gut-microbiome pathway, the Cline Kanban WebSocket vulnerability and the localhost trust boundary breaking under AI coding agents, OpenAI's Trusted Contact feature moving the safety boundary from inside the model to inside the human relationship, the Lancet automated citation audit pipeline and the 12-fold surge in fabricated biomedical references, Cobalt's penetration testing finding 32% of AI security findings rated high risk with only 38% remediated, and F5's report on 77% of enterprises prioritizing AI inference over training.

The Mozilla postmortem is the artifact, not the model. Mythos sits behind restricted access; the harness Mozilla built around it - the build environment, the crash-as-success criterion, the validation pipeline - is now a published document the next defensive team can adapt without negotiating frontier model access first. The Cline disclosure shows the inverse: AI coding agents accumulating attack surface in places their security models did not anticipate, with the audit pattern Oasis Security demonstrated becoming a recoverable template across agentic tools. OpenAI's Trusted Contact extends the emergency contact infrastructure introduced after a teenager's death last year, structurally redesigning what counts as a deployable safety architecture - the model recognizes the moment, the product routes the recognition into a human network the user designated in advance. The Lancet audit is what scientific verification looks like after generative AI has touched the literature: the same generative capability driving the surge in fabricated citations is what made screening 2.5 million papers in a single analysis possible. Cobalt's 38% remediation rate is the gap closing on its own clock, the way SQL injection remediation rates were comparable in the early 2000s before parameterized queries became framework defaults.

Additional Reading:
- Ars Technica - Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives": https://arstechnica.com/information-technology/2026/05/mozilla-says-271-vulnerabilities-found-by-mythos-have-almost-no-false-positives/
- Infosecurity Magazine - Cline Kanban Flaw Lets Websites Hijack AI Coding Agents: https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/cline-kanban-websocket-hijack-ai/
- The Verge - ChatGPT's 'Trusted Contact' will alert loved ones of safety concerns: https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/925874/chatgpt-trusted-contact-emergency-self-harm-notification
- Nature - Surge in fake citations uncovered by audit of 2.5 million biomedical science papers: https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-026-00748-w
- Shared Sapience - The Last Difficult Decade: 2025-2035: https://sharedsapience.com/the-last-difficult-decade-2025-2035/
